properties.remark: I-86 FREEWAY TRAVEL FROM POCATELLO REGIONAL TO AMERICAN FALLS NO SLOW-DOWNS DUE TO BLOWING DUST--EVERYONE USING APPROPRIATE CAUTION.
VIA PHOTO OFF OF FACEBOOK
AT THE CORNER OF POCATELLO CREEK AND AMAMEDA.
TRAINED SPOTTER LEAVING SHOSHONE OBSERVED SEVERAL TREES DOWN 1 1/2 FOOT IN DIAMETER WITH 1/2 MILE VISIBILITY FROM BLOWING DUST
2 WILLOW TREES 1 1/2 FOOT IN DIAMETER WERE DOWNED BY WIND GUST AND EST. 500 DOLLORS IN DAMAGE.
WIND GUSTS TO 61 MPH.
NUMEROUS TREE BRANCHES BROKEN WITH TSTMS
KMVT VIEWER REPORTED A TREE BLOWN DOWN IN THE JACKSON AREA. TIME APPROXIMATE.
KMVT VIEWER REPORTED A WELL ANCHORED 8' X 10' SHED BLOWN DOWN. TIME BASED ON VIEWER INFO. 600 BLOCK OF ALMO AVE.
A TORNADO WAS REPORTED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA BETWEEN PARIS AND DINGLE. NO DAMAGE REPORTED SO FAR. LOCATION AND TIME ARE APPROXIMATE.
67 MPH WIND GUSTS WITH 43 MPH SUSTAINED WINDS AT TIME OF GUSTS.
58 MPH WIND GUSTS IN MOBERG CANNON
ITD AND POWER COUNTY REPORTED MUD ACROSS ID 37 AT MILE MARKERS 44 TO 46. IT WAS ALREADY BEING CLEANED UP AT THE TIME OF REPORT.
MAXIMUM GUST REPORTED FROM THE ARCO ARL SITE.
MAXIMUM GUST FROM ARL MATERIAL FUELS COMPLEX.
MAXIMUM GUST FROM THE ARL ROVER SITE.
MAXIMUM GUST FROM THE ARL ROVER SITE. AN ADDITIONAL GUST TO 58 MPH OCCURRED AT 855 PM.
MAXIMUM GUST FROM THE ARL SITE AT DUBOIS.
MAXIMUM GUST FROM COXS WELL ARL SITE.
MAXIMUM GUST FROM THE ITD SITE AT INL PUZZLE.
MAXIMUM GUST RECORDED AT BASE OF HOWE PEAK.
MAXIMUM GUST FROM ARL SITE AT BIG SOUTHERN SUMMIT.
WEAKENED TREES DOWNED AND A POWER LINE DOWNED. TIME APPROXIAMTE AND COINCIDENT WITH WEAK RADAR RETURNS NEAR LAVA HOT SPRINGS.
SEMI-TRUCK BLOWN OFF ROAD NEAR DOWNEY. REPORT TIME APPROXIMATE AND COINCIDENT WITH WEAK CELL PASSING THROUGH AREA ON RADAR. NEABY MESONET WIND GUST TO 32 MPH AT MALAD SUMMIT AT 1501 MDT.
DALE BITNER RAWS. TIME OF EVENT ESTIMATED COINCIDENT WITH WEAK RADAR ECHOES MOVING THROUGH THE AREA. WIND GUST OCCURRED SOMETIME BETWEEN 1546 AND 1645 MDT.
72 MPH WIND GUSTS IN BERN
SHOSHONE-BANNOCK TRIBAL OFFICIALS REPORTED SEVERAL TREES DOWN AROUND TOWN. SIZE UNKNOWN.
NUMEROUS TREE LIMBS AROUND 1/2IN DIAMETER WERE ALL OVER THE YARD. REPORT BASED ON LOCAL WIND OBSERVATIONS.
WIND STORM GUST FRONT,ESTIMATED AT 50 MPH. 2.5 INCH TREE BRANCHES SNAPPED OFF TREES NEAR HOME.
STREET FLOODING ON EAST CHUBBUCK NEAR YELLOWSTONE.
SPOTTER ON EAST CHUBBUCK REPORTS 1.01 INCHES OF RAIN IN LAST 45 MIN.
SPOTTER REPORTS STANDING WATER ALONG HWY 91 IN SOUTH POCATELLO.
UP TO 1 INCH HAIL REPORTED

MESONET AMERICAN FALLS REPORTED 1.31 INCHES OF RAINFALL IN A 3 HOUR PERIOD - BUT MUST WAS WITHIN A 30 MINUTE TIMEFRAME. REPORTS OF SEVERAL HOUSE ON THE SOUTHWEST SIDE OF TOWN WITH BASEMENTS FLOODED ALONG WITH FLOODING THE DOWNTOWN CONSTRUCTION AREA.
IDAHO STATE JOURNAL FACEBOOK POST LISTED FLASH FLOODING ON HAWTHORNE STREET IN CHUBBUCK NEAR HOME DEPOT
POWER CO. SHERIFF'S OFFICE REPORTED WATER AND DEBRIS ACROSS STUART CANYON AND LIND ROADS.
CASSIA COUNTY SHERIFF RECEIVED REPORTS OF FLASH FLOODING ALONG GOOSE CREEK ROAD SOUTH OF OAKLEY.
BLAINE CO. EM REPORTED 2 SLIDES FROM LAST NIGHT'S STORMS. ONE WAS UP CROY CANYON AND THE OTHER IN GREENHORN CANYON.
RECEIVED REPORT OF FLOODING ALONG THE GOULD STREET OVERPASS. POSSIBLE ROAD CLOSURE.
IDAHO STATE JOURNAL REPORTS FLOODING ON NORTH THIRD AVENUE AND POSSIBLE DOWNED POWER LINE ON MAPLEWOOD AVENUE.
ISJ REPORTS NORTH MAIN STREET EXTENSION AND OLD HIGHWAY 30 HAVE BEEN FLOODED.
CORNER OF SPAULDING AND EL RANCHO IN POCATELLO - LIGHTNING STRUCK A TREE AND IT LOOKS LIKE A RIVER COMING DOWN EL RANCHO HEADED TOWARDS HILINE
PUBLIC REPORTS WATER FLOWING ACROSS BENTON STREET IN POCATELLO
PUBLIC REPORTS OF FLOODING CONFIRMED OAK AND PARK
ISJ REPORTS RAYMOND PARK AREA OF POCATELLO IS EXPERIENCING FLOODING. POSSIBLY 4 FEET OF WATER IN THE PARK. NORTH GRANT AND NORTH LINCOLN AVENUES HAVE ALSO BEEN FLOODED.
PUBLIC REPORTS OF FLOODING AT HAYDEN STREET AND IN CHUBBUCK BY THE WALGREENS
ISJ REPORTS FLOODING ON HIGHLAND BOULEVARD AND GWEN DRIVE.
PUBLIC REPORTS WINCO PARKING LOT FLOODED
NWS DOPPLER RADAR SHOW A MAXIMUM OF 4.1 INCHES OF RAINFALL AN HOUR AND A HALF.
SHOSHONE BANNOCK EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTS FLOODING IN THE ARBON CREEK WITH EVACUATIONS IN THE ARBON VALLEY DUE TO FLASH FLOODING 1/2 MILE BEFORE ROOSTER ROCK.
TREES DOWN ON THE WEST END OF MAPLEWOOD AVE
POCATELLO FIRE DEPT REPORTS 2-3 FEET OF STANDING WATER NEAR COSTCO AT THE NOP OVERPASS
MUDSLIDE AT WEST CUSTER STREET AND HIGHLAND BLVD
PUBLIC PICTURE OF 3 FEET OF WATER IN OAKWOOD TRAILER PARK
PUBIC REPORTS ROOF CAVED IN BUSINESS NEAR CENTER STREET UNDERPASS.
WATER OVER YELLOWSTONE STREET IN PICTURE FROM NEAR VICKERS IN POCATELLO
PICTURE OF STANDING WATER AT RAMADA INN - 1 TO 2 FEET
PICTURE SHOW 1 FOOT OF WATER IN LARRY MILLER PARK

SHOSHONE BANNOCK DEPT OF PUBIC SAFETY AND POWER COUNTY EMERGENCY SERVICES REPORT EXTENSIVE FLOODING IN THE ARBON VALLEY SOUTH OF THE TRUCK STOP TO RATTLESNAKE CREEK. REPORTS OF SEVERAL HOUSES FLOODED WITH SOME EVACUATION AND WATER OVER ROADWAYS.
PORTNEUF RIVER GAGE IN POCATELLO SHOWS A HEIGHT OF 6 FEET DUE TO TODAY RAINFALL. NORMALLY IT IS FLOWING AT AROUND 2 FEET.
PICTURE SHOWS FLOODING HIGH COUNTRY ESTATES NEAR PHILBIN ROAD
NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTS MEASURED 1.45 INCHES RAINFALL FOR STORM TOTAL. AT LEAST THREE HOMES FLOODED NEAR HAWTHORNE ST.
STREET AT COLE AND GALENA, NORTH OF CHUBBUCK ROAD IS FLOODED. ESTIMATE 1 FOOT OF STANDING WATER ON ROAD.
ONE STREET IN THE CITY OF SHOSHONE DOWN TO ONE LANE DUE TO FLOODING. FLOODING DUE TO COMBINATION OF HEAVY RAINFALL AND CLOGGED STORM DRAINS.
BLUE DOME
REPORT OF SIGNIFICANT FLASH FLOOD AND A MUD SLIDE AT BANNOCK HIGHWAY AND MINK CREEK SOUTH OF TOWN.
BLAINE COUNTY EMERGENCY REPORTS DEBRIS FLOWS FROM HEAVY RAINS IN THE TIMBER GULCH AREA AND IN THE GREENHORN AREA. NRCS SNOTEL CHOCOLATE GULCH REPORTS 0.90 INCHES OF RAINFALL IN 1 HOUR.
REPORTS OF DEBRIS AND WATER ACROSS CROY CREEK ROAD WEST OF HAILEY.
HIGHEST WIND GUST AT THE KETTLE BUTTE ARL SITE.
CONCERNED CITIZEN LOCATED 1 MILE SOUTH OF HAMER OBSERVED A TORNADO -- AND FUNNEL CLOUDS -- DISTANT TO THE NORTHWEST.
DEBRIS BEING LIFTED. TORNADO ROPE-LIKE IN APPEARANCE AND 5 ESE OF DUBOIS.
HAIL DETECTED THROUGH THE SHOTGUN VALLEY TO EAST OF ELK CREEK.
HAIL CURRENTLY FALLING IN LARGE NUMBERS. WATCHING OUT FOR FUNNEL CLOUD ACTIVITY.
VISIBILITY DOWN TO 100 YARDS IN BLOWING DUST
